Speedup: 0.008941%                                                                                       \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. Speedup: 0.02364%                                                                                        \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. Speedup: 0.05622%                                                                                        \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. As a   \n          consequence, 11.8x the data written to the L2 cache has to be communicated to the L2 Compression unit. Try    \n          maximizing local coherence for the write operations to compressible memory. For example, avoid writes with    \n          large strides as they lead to partial accesses to many L2 cache lines. Speedup: 0.008715%                                                                                       \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. As a   \n          consequence, 6.1x the data written to the L2 cache has to be communicated to the L2 Compression unit. Try     \n          maximizing local coherence for the write operations to compressible memory. For example, avoid writes with    \n          large strides as they lead to partial accesses to many L2 cache lines. Speedup: 0.02427%                                                                                        \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. As a   \n          consequence, 4.5x the data written to the L2 cache has to be communicated to the L2 Compression unit. Try     \n          maximizing local coherence for the write operations to compressible memory. For example, avoid writes with    \n          large strides as they lead to partial accesses to many L2 cache lines. Try changing the number of threads per block to be a multiple of 32 threads. Between 128 and 256      \n          threads per block is a good initial range for experimentation. Use smaller thread blocks rather than one      \n          large thread block per multiprocessor if latency affects performance.  This is particularly beneficial to     \n          kernels that frequently call __syncthreads(). See the Hardware Model                                          \n          (https://docs.nvidia.com/nsight-compute/ProfilingGuide/index.html#metrics-hw-model) description for more      \n          details on launch configurations.                                                                             \n    ----- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    OPT   Est.
